verses.1: When you sit to eat with a ruler,
verses.2: put a knife to your throat
verses.3: Don’t be desirous of his dainties,
verses.4: Don’t weary yourself to be rich.
verses.5: Why do you set your eyes on that which is not?
verses.6: Don’t eat the food of him who has a stingy eye,
verses.7: for as he thinks about the cost, so he is.
verses.8: You will vomit up the morsel which you have eaten
verses.9: Don’t speak in the ears of a fool,
verses.10: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone.
verses.13: Don’t withhold correction from a child.
verses.15: My son, if your heart is wise,
verses.17: Don’t let your heart envy sinners,
verses.18: Indeed surely there is a future hope,
verses.19: Listen, my son, and be wise,
verses.20: Don’t be among ones drinking too much wine,
verses.21: for the drunkard and the glutton shall become poor;
verses.22: Listen to your father who gave you life,
verses.23: Buy the truth, and don’t sell it.
verses.24: The father of the righteous has great joy.
verses.25: Let your father and your mother be glad!
verses.26: My son, give me your heart;
verses.27: For a prostitute is a deep pit;
verses.28: Yes, she lies in wait like a robber,
verses.30: Those who stay long at the wine;
verses.31: Don’t look at the wine when it is red,
verses.32: In the end, it bites like a snake,
verses.33: Your eyes will see strange things,
verses.34: Yes, you will be as he who lies down in the middle of the sea,
verses.35: “They hit me, and I was not hurt!
